Off Calendar Sep-06-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT TURNER & TOWNSEND, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Production Of Plaintiffs Documents.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Bastine, a former employee of defendant Turner & Townsend, sues for employment discrimination and retaliation. Defendants demanded production of plaintiffs' documents at his deposition, particularly including "All DOCUMENTS reflecting any COMMUNICATION between YOU and any person or entity concerning any of YOUR claims in the COMPLAINT . . .." Plaintiff initially produced certain texts he had exchanged with Kearra Taylor, another employee of defendant Turner & Townsend.

During plaintiff's deposition, counsel for defendants attempted to broaden the scope of documents requested to the entirety of plaintiff's email or text chains with Ms. Taylor without limitation as to subject matter. Similarly, in meet and confer communications subsequent to the deposition, defendants' counsel sought "all communications" with Ms. Taylor and not just those concerning plaintiff's claims in the Complaint. Plaintiff took the position that he had produced what was requested, all communications with Ms. Taylor concerning his claims in the Complaint.

Subsequently, defendants propounded a Second Request for Production of Documents which more broadly sought communications between plaintiff and Ms. Taylor, specifically those which "relate to your or her employment with Townsend & Townsend, Inc, and/or your lawsuit or plan to file a lawsuit against the Defendants." In response to this broadened request, the plaintiff produced more correspondence with Ms. Taylor.

Defendants moved to compel, again contending that they wanted all correspondence with Ms. Taylor, ignoring the original limitation of their document demand to communications concerning plaintiff's claims in the Complaint and their later one to communications concerning plaintiff's and Ms. Taylor's employment with defendant Turner & Townsend and plaintiff's plans to sue.

Defendants argue that there are time gaps in the produced messages with Ms. Taylor and unexplained redactions therein. Defendants claimed to demonstrate the redactions by attachments to the Declaration of Saisruth Paspulati. However, defendants failed to attach anything to the Declaration, either in the courtesy copies provided to the Court or the filed copy available on the Register of Actions. Moreover, plaintiff continued to assert that he had produced all documents described in either of defendants' document demands.

Defendants' informal attempts to broaden their document demands are legally unsupported. Further, the factually unsupported assertions in their motion do not establish that plaintiff has withheld or improperly redacted any documents within the parameters of defendants' requests. Their motion must be denied.

Plaintiff permissibly sought the award of sanctions in his opposition to the motion but did not state the amount or "person, party, or attorney against whom the sanction is sought", as required. CCP sec. 2023.040. Sanctions must therefore be denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is directed to submit a proposed order in Word format to Temporary Judge James H. Fleming via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with copies to all parties.
